hard_constraints.continuity_rule: exact same mechanism state before resume
hard_constraints.search_style: small interpretable two-anchor blend set only

candidate_specs.name: twoanchor_l10_zpair_neg4_then_ypair_neg2_plus_l11_y10x0back2
candidate_specs.rationale: Keep the proven anchor10 z4+y2 hybrid, but replace late11 maxbest with the softer resume-side y10x0back2 block to recover mean while keeping the targeted residual pressure.

candidate_specs.name: twoanchor_l10_ypair_neg2_then_zpair_neg4_plus_l11_y10x0back2
candidate_specs.rationale: Same strong hybrid ingredients as above, but finish anchor10 with the z-pair before resuming, to bias toward protecting xz / Ka2_z while keeping the softer late11 block.

candidate_specs.name: twoanchor_l10_zpair_neg4_then_ypair_neg2_plus_l11_bestmean
candidate_specs.rationale: Keep the anchor10 z4+y2 hybrid, but swap late11 all the way back to bestmean. This tests whether anchor10 alone carries most of the Ka2_y suppression.

candidate_specs.name: twoanchor_l10_ypair_neg2_then_zpair_neg4_plus_l11_bestmean
candidate_specs.rationale: Reverse-order counterpart of the z4+y2 + bestmean blend, to test whether ending anchor10 with the z-pair gives a cleaner unified trade.

candidate_specs.name: twoanchor_l10_zpair_neg6_then_ypair_neg1_plus_l11_y10x0back2
candidate_specs.rationale: Minimal-anchor10 blend around the best-mean family: keep the strong neg6 z-pair, add only a 1 s y-pair after it, then use the already-proven y10x0back2 late11 block.

candidate_specs.name: twoanchor_l10_ypair_neg1_then_zpair_neg6_plus_l11_y10x0back2
candidate_specs.rationale: Same minimal 1 s anchor10 y-dose as above, but place it before the neg6 z-pair to test whether the order changes how much mean recovery survives.

candidate_specs.name: twoanchor_l10_zpair_neg5_then_ypair_neg1_plus_l11_y10x0back2
candidate_specs.rationale: Interpolated unified blend: soften anchor10 z-dwell from 6 s to 5 s, keep only a 1 s y-pair, and keep the resume-side late11 y10x0back2 block. This is the most literal blend between the best-mean family and the mild residual hybrid.

candidate_specs.name: twoanchor_l10_ypair_neg1_then_zpair_neg5_plus_l11_y10x0back2
candidate_specs.rationale: Reverse-order counterpart of the z5+y1 minimal blend, included to see whether ending anchor10 with the z-pair is still preferable at the 5 s z-dwell level.

bottom_line.verdict: twoanchor_l10_zpair_neg5_then_ypair_neg1_plus_l11_y10x0back2 is a real unified frontier win: it reaches 9.923 / 1.026 / 99.633, improving the old best-mean anchor by 0.260 mean-points and 0.081 max-points simultaneously.

scientific_conclusion: The unification pass succeeded. The key structural lesson is that the best compromise did not come from the heavier z4+y2 hybrid; it came from a milder anchor10 interpolation: twoanchor_l10_zpair_neg5_then_ypair_neg1_plus_l11_y10x0back2. That z5+y1 minimal blend with late11 y10x0back2 improves dKa_yy (82.609), dKg_zz (59.017), Ka2_y (99.633), and Ka2_z (0.842) relative to the old best-mean anchor, while also moving the mean/max pair to a strictly better location. The stronger z4+y2 blends still define a useful lower-max subfrontier, with twoanchor_l10_zpair_neg4_then_ypair_neg2_plus_l11_y10x0back2 reaching 99.598, but they no longer look like the best single unified choice because they give back too much mean.
